titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Collection of Bookplates by John W. Evans
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Arts Library Special Collections staff

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday August 7, 2018 at 18:53

did.unittitle: Collection of Bookplates by John W. Evans
did.abstract: This collection includes bookplates designed by John W. Evans (1855-1943), a master wood engraver born in Brooklyn, NY.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Yale Bookplate Collection was formerly housed in Sterling Memorial Library. The collection was incorporated into the Arts of the Book Collection in 1984 and then moved to the Seeley G. Mudd Library in 1990. In 2011, it was transferred to the Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Collection of Bookplates by John W. Evans, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection was removed from filing cabinets which housed bookplates by individual artists.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: John William Evans (1855-1943) was an American engraver and graphic artist born in Brooklyn, NY. A master wood engraver, Evans created reproductions of famous paintings and other works to illustrate magazines such as Century , Harper's , and Scribner's . In "retirement," Evans took up bookplate design. His award-winning wood engraving was exhibited in the Paris Exposition of 1900, the Chicago Exposition of 1903, and the Panama Exposition of 1915. Evans had close associations with other bookplate artists, including Timothy Cole and William Fowler Hopson, who was Evans's brother-in-law. He was also a longtime and active member of the Freemasons.
Reference: "John W. Evans, at 84, Still Active Master of Wood Engraving Craft," Brooklyn Daily Eagle , February 20, 1938.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection includes a number of bookplates designed by John W. Evans (1855-1943), a master wood engraver born in Brooklyn, NY. The bookplates were designed primarily for private individuals, including several for his friends and family. The collection also includes four woodblocks for Evans's bookplate designs and a letter written by Evans to his sister, Ada Hopson.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Bookplates. II. Correspondence and Ephemera. III. Woodblocks.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged numerically, according to accompanying checklist.

did.unittitle: #9. Randolph Macon Women's College (Louise Jordan Smith)
#15. Cornelia Eames and Frank Dell Anthony
#27. Therese, Rosemary, and John W. Evans, Jr.
#30. Audubon Club of Erasmus High School
#36. Edna May and Robert A. Hutzelman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This folder includes an illustration for a Christmas / New Year's greeting card.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The folder contains the following: a piece of correspondence between John W. Evans and Ada Hopson (undated); a newspaper article about John W. Evans published in the Brooklyn Daily Eagle , February 20, 1938; and a handwritten checklist of the materials in the collection.

did.unittitle: Evans bookplate for Mrs. W.F.H.; wood engraving.
Evans wood block bookplate for W.F.H.
